generatedSentence: In the vast, icebound acreage surrounding the old church, once an embassy, a callow pilgrim-wizard, known for his somewhat oafish demeanor, was often found napping by a handmade ebonite backboard, his lifelong journey a constant thought. He had a fearful outlook, burdened by a flashback of a past mistake, yet he acknowledged his duty, as the grounds were slowly being overtaken by a rubbery, knobbly monstrosity which needed to be described and understood, lest its unknown evils forever permeate their world. The wizard, though often seen as an uninitiated napper, had a keen eye, a deadeye aim with his energy staff, and a temper that could flare up, especially when facing the grabby extensions of the looming threat. His headquarters, once a quiet retreat where he enjoyed java and pawpaws from a samovar, now felt like a liability, particularly as the monstrosity seemed to victimize everything with an unrealistic, almost mocking, presence. He knew he couldn't abbreviate the problem; it was a complex situation, a variable beast, and he had to be brave, avoiding a downhill slide into despair, while a distant windrow of unusual rock formations hinted at something more sinister. The scout, an uppity jennet, came galloping to the wizard’s hardtop, its muffler sputtering, warning of the immediate danger as the prowler, a grotesque new iteration of the monstrosity, latched onto the church's ancient reactor. The wizard, with his bold ink outlines and cinematic lighting, knew this was the climax; he had to inject a solution. He kissed a small, brave marmot he kept, then, with a knockout punch of magic, whisked himself to the pit, bypassing the noisy jetfoil, ready to confront the problem, saving what he could from this potential nuclear robbery and the terrible outcome it promised. Through a swirling diptych of energy, the wizard, now a true victor and no longer a victim, had pushed back the threat, securing the church grounds. He had enjoined a new union between the various denominations, proving that even a naughty, callow individual could rise to the occasion. As the moonshine glowed softly over the now peaceful acreage, an electrician arrived, nodding his approval, knowing the reactor was safe. The wizard, having fulfilled his destiny, now looked forward to a renewed quietude, perhaps even a windsurf on a calm lake, his journey complete, leaving behind the throwaway jokes and past fears, securing a peaceful resolution for all.

rawPanelSentences: In the vast, icebound acreage surrounding the old church, once an embassy, a callow pilgrim-wizard, known for his somewhat oafish demeanor, was often found napping by a handmade ebonite backboard, his lifelong journey a constant thought. He had a fearful outlook, burdened by a flashback of a past mistake, yet he acknowledged his duty, as the grounds were slowly being overtaken by a rubbery, knobbly monstrosity which needed to be described and understood, lest its unknown evils forever permeate their world.

The wizard, though often seen as an uninitiated napper, had a keen eye, a deadeye aim with his energy staff, and a temper that could flare up, especially when facing the grabby extensions of the looming threat. His headquarters, once a quiet retreat where he enjoyed java and pawpaws from a samovar, now felt like a liability, particularly as the monstrosity seemed to victimize everything with an unrealistic, almost mocking, presence. He knew he couldn't abbreviate the problem; it was a complex situation, a variable beast, and he had to be brave, avoiding a downhill slide into despair, while a distant windrow of unusual rock formations hinted at something more sinister.

The scout, an uppity jennet, came galloping to the wizard’s hardtop, its muffler sputtering, warning of the immediate danger as the prowler, a grotesque new iteration of the monstrosity, latched onto the church's ancient reactor. The wizard, with his bold ink outlines and cinematic lighting, knew this was the climax; he had to inject a solution. He kissed a small, brave marmot he kept, then, with a knockout punch of magic, whisked himself to the pit, bypassing the noisy jetfoil, ready to confront the problem, saving what he could from this potential nuclear robbery and the terrible outcome it promised.

Through a swirling diptych of energy, the wizard, now a true victor and no longer a victim, had pushed back the threat, securing the church grounds. He had enjoined a new union between the various denominations, proving that even a naughty, callow individual could rise to the occasion. As the moonshine glowed softly over the now peaceful acreage, an electrician arrived, nodding his approval, knowing the reactor was safe. The wizard, having fulfilled his destiny, now looked forward to a renewed quietude, perhaps even a windsurf on a calm lake, his journey complete, leaving behind the throwaway jokes and past fears, securing a peaceful resolution for all.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a wise-eyed wizard with a glowing staff and a tattered, flowing emerald robe. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone wizard stands on a desolate, icebound plain, gazing at a distant, menacing, ruberry-knobbly monstrosity encroaching upon an ancient, futuristic church. Extreme wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The wizard, looking determined, holds up his glowing staff, deflecting a shadowy, tendril-like extension from the monstrosity that is attempting to breach the church's perimeter. Medium shot, dynamic angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The wizard, mid-air in a powerful jump, brandishes his staff with a bright, energetic burst, directly engaging the core of the glowing, unstable reactor as the monstrosity writhes in pain below him. Low angle, action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The wizard stands victorious on the now-calm church grounds, the monstrosity receding into the distance, as a small, glowing marmot sits peacefully on his shoulder. Wide shot, serene atmosphere.

definitions.definition: An organism showing abnormal development or deformity.

An error; a blunder.
A pitch which was intended to be pitched in a hard-to-hit location, but instead ends up in an easy-to-hit place.

definitions.definition: To misunderstand (someone).
To commit an unintentional error; to do or think something wrong.
To take or choose wrongly.
Part of the exhaust pipe of a car that dampens the noise the engine produces.
A silencer or suppressor fitted to a gun.
A type of scarf.
The light of the moon; moonlight.
High-proof alcohol (especially whiskey) that is often, but not always, produced illegally.

definitions.definition: Nonsense.

definitions.definition: A branch of pure mathematics relating the Monster group to an invariant of elliptic functions.
A spiced dish of eggs and fried onions.
A month.
A Christian house of worship; a building where Christian religious services take place.

definitions.definition: Christians collectively seen as a single spiritual community; Christianity.

To educate someone religiously, as in in a church.

The function or duty of an ambassador.
An organization or group of officials who permanently represent a sovereign state in a second sovereign state or with respect to an international organization such as the United Nations.

definitions.definition: A temporary mission representing a sovereign state.
The official residence of such a group, or of an ambassador.
Moral badness; wickedness; malevolence; the forces or behaviors that are the opposite or enemy of good.
definitions.example: Evil lacks spirituality, hence its need for mind control.

definitions.definition: Something which impairs the happiness of a being or deprives a being of any good; something which causes suffering of any kind to sentient beings; harm; injury; mischief.
A malady or disease; especially in the phrase king's evil (scrofula).
Frightening.
Tending to fear; timid.

definitions.definition: Terrible; shockingly bad.
Frightened; filled with terror.
Extremely; fearfully.
An extremely long time.

definitions.definition: A mythical time in the infinite future that will never come.
definitions.example: Sure, I'd be happy to meet with you on the 12th of forever.

definitions.definition: Permanent, lasting
(duration) For all time, for all eternity; for a lifetime; for an infinite amount of time.

definitions.definition: (duration) For a very long time, a seeming eternity.
definitions.example: We had to wait forever to get inside.

definitions.definition: (frequency) Constantly or frequently.

definitions.definition: To admit the knowledge of; to recognize as a fact or truth; to declare one's belief in
definitions.example: to acknowledge the being of a god

definitions.definition: To own or recognize in a particular quality, character or relationship; to admit the claims or authority of; to give recognition to.
To be grateful of (e.g. a benefit or a favour)

definitions.definition: To report (the receipt of a message to its sender).
definitions.example: This is to acknowledge your kind invitation to participate in the upcoming debate.

definitions.definition: To own as genuine or valid; to assent to (a legal instrument) to give it validity; to avow or admit in legal form.
Generally accepted, recognized or admitted.
Other than supposed; different.
definitions.example: He said he didn’t do it, but the evidence was otherwise.

definitions.definition: (manner) Differently, in another way.
definitions.example: Could I do otherwise than smile?.

definitions.definition: In different circumstances; or else.
definitions.example: I’m not well today, otherwise I would have helped.  You have to open your umbrella, otherwise you'll get wet.

definitions.definition: In all other respects.
definitions.example: He lost his temper once in a while. Otherwise he behaved rationally.

definitions.definition: A passenger-carrying hydrofoil propelled by water jets.
A blend of coffee imported from the island of Java.
Coffee in general.
A dance popular in France in the early 20th century.
Tending to grab, especially rudely or greedily.
To prevent harm or difficulty.
To put aside, to avoid.
A reduction in cost or expenditure.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) Something (usually money) that is saved, particularly money that has been set aside for the future.
definitions.example: I invested all my savings in gold.

definitions.definition: The action of the verb to save.
Exception; reservation
That saves someone from damnation; redemptive.
Preserving; rescuing.
Thrifty; frugal.

definitions.definition: Bringing back in returns or in receipts the sum expended; incurring no loss, though not gainful.

definitions.definition: Making reservation or exception.

definitions.definition: (in compound adjectives) relating to making a saving: e.g. labour-saving, energy-saving light bulbs.
With the exception of; except; save.
Without disrespect to.
Someone, usually male, who uses (or has skill with) magic, mystic items, and magical and mystical practices.
One who is especially skilled or unusually talented in a particular field.

definitions.definition: A computer program or script used to simplify complex operations, often for an inexperienced user.

An adult virgin over the age of 30.
To practice wizardry.
To conjure.
Fine, superb (originally RAF slang).

definitions.definition: To move lightly and nimbly.

definitions.definition: The undomesticated state of a wild animal

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural) a wilderness
To commit random acts of assault, robbery, and rape in an urban setting, especially as a gang.
Untamed; not domesticated; specifically, in an unbroken line of undomesticated animals (as opposed to feral, referring to undomesticated animals whose ancestors were domesticated).

definitions.definition: From or relating to wild creatures.

definitions.definition: Unrestrained or uninhibited.

definitions.definition: Raucous, unruly, or licentious.

definitions.definition: Disheveled, tangled, or untidy.

definitions.definition: Enthusiastic.

definitions.definition: Exposed to the wind and sea; unsheltered.

definitions.definition: Hard to steer; said of a vessel.
(of a knot) Not capable of being represented as a finite closed polygonal chain.
Amazing, awesome, unbelievable.

definitions.definition: Able to stand in for others, e.g. a card in games, or a text character in computer pattern matching.
definitions.example: In this card game, aces are wild: they can take the place of any other card.

definitions.definition: Inaccurately; not on target.
definitions.example: The javelin flew wild and struck a spectator, to the horror of all observing.

To moderate or control.

definitions.example: Tempering is a heat treatment technique applied to metals, alloys, and glass to achieve greater toughness by increasing the strength of materials and/or ductility. Tempering is performed by a controlled reheating of the work piece to a temperature below its lower eutectic critical temperature.

To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.

To treat with fondness.
The act of making someone unconscious, or at least unable to come back on their feet within a certain period of time; a TKO.

Rendering someone unconscious.

To make, or cause to make (the recovery), with a certain hurried or unskillful forward swing of the body.

definitions.example: Three is a possible outcome of tossing a six-sided die.

definitions.definition: The anticipated or desired results or evidence of a learning experience (often used in the phrase learning outcomes).

definitions.definition: The scoreline; the result.

An amusing story.
Something said or done for amusement, not in seriousness.

A laughably worthless thing or person; a sham.

definitions.definition: To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.
definitions.example: I didn’t mean what I said — I was only joking.

definitions.definition: Really good.
Funny or entertaining.

definitions.definition: A female ass or donkey; a jenny.
A small Spanish horse.
A garment knitted from wool, worn over the upper body.
A shirt worn by a member of an athletic team, usually oversized, typically depicting the athlete's name and team number as well as the team's logotype.
A type of fabric knit

A metal urn with a spigot, for boiling water for making tea. Traditionally, the water is heated by hot coals or charcoal in a chimney-like tube which runs through the center of the urn. Today, it is more likely that the water is heated by an electric coil.
A person sent out to gain and bring in tidings; especially, one employed in war to gain information about the enemy and ground.
An act of scouting or reconnoitering.
A member of any number of youth organizations belonging to the international scout movement, such as the Boy Scouts of America or Girl Scouts of the United States.
A person who assesses and/or recruits others; especially, one who identifies promising talent on behalf of a sports team.

A college servant (in Oxford, England or Yale or Harvard), originally implying a male servant, attending to (usually several) students or undergraduates in a variety of ways that includes cleaning; corresponding to the duties of a gyp or possibly bedder at Cambridge University; and at Dublin, a skip.
A fielder in a game for practice.
(up until 1920s) A fighter aircraft.
Term of address for a man or boy.
To explore a wide terrain, as if on a search; to reconnoiter.
To observe, watch, or look for, as a scout; to follow for the purpose of observation, as a scout.

definitions.example: Two drivers have already gone into the pit this early in the race.

definitions.definition: A section of the marching band containing mallet percussion instruments and other large percussion instruments too large to march, such as the tam tam. Also, the area on the sidelines where these instruments are placed.
A mine.

A hole or trench in the ground, excavated according to grid coordinates, so that the provenance of any feature observed and any specimen or artifact revealed may be established by precise measurement.
A trading pit.
The bottom part of something.
definitions.example: I felt pain in the pit of my stomach.

definitions.definition: Armpit.
A luggage hold.
A small surface hole or depression, a fossa.
The indented mark left by a pustule, as in smallpox.
The grave, or underworld.
An enclosed area into which gamecocks, dogs, and other animals are brought to fight, or where dogs are trained to kill rats.

Formerly, that part of a theatre, on the floor of the house, below the level of the stage and behind the orchestra; now, in England, commonly the part behind the stalls; in the United States, the parquet; also, the occupants of such a part of a theatre.
Part of a casino which typically holds tables for blackjack, craps, roulette, and other games.
A pit bull terrier.
definitions.example: I'm taking one of my pits to the vet on Thursday.

definitions.definition: A mosh pit.
definitions.example: Because the museum was closed for renovation, the school decided to bring its fourth-graders to the pit at a Cannibal Corpse gig instead.

definitions.definition: (law enforcement, usually used with "maneuver") A maneuver by which a police officer, by use of a police car, nudges the vehicle of a fleeing suspect enough for the suspect's vehicle to lose control and become disabled so the police officer can catch and apprehend the suspect.
The fissile core of a nuclear weapon, commonly made of plutonium surrounded by high-explosive lenses.
(hospital slang) The emergency department.
To make pits in; to mark with little hollows.
definitions.example: Exposure to acid rain pitted the metal.

definitions.definition: To put (an animal) into a pit for fighting.
To bring (something) into opposition with something else.
definitions.example: Are you ready to pit your wits against one of the world's greatest puzzles?

definitions.definition: To return to the pits during a race for refuelling, tyre changes, repairs etc.
To push or pump (something, especially fluids) into a cavity or passage.
definitions.example: The nurse injected a painkilling drug into the veins of my forearm.

definitions.definition: To introduce (something) suddenly or violently.
definitions.example: Punk injected a much-needed sense of urgency into the British music scene.

definitions.definition: To administer an injection to (someone or something), especially of medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: Now lie back while we inject you with the anesthetic.

definitions.definition: To take or be administered something by means of injection, especially medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: It's been a week since I stopped injecting, and I'm still in withdrawal.

definitions.definition: To introduce (code) into an existing program or its memory space, often without tight integration and sometimes through a security vulnerability.
To cast or throw; used with on.
A conference at which participants of similar status discuss and exchange views.
A television show segment in which pundits or reporters discuss current events.
Of, relating to, or resembling rubber, especially in consistency.
definitions.example: What a bad restaurant! The beef was so rubbery I thought I'd never finish chewing it.

definitions.definition: Something temporary and disposable.
Disposable; intended for a single use prior to being discarded.
definitions.example: As the film opens, a martial-drum-dominated version of the "Star Wars" theme and the inevitable bottom-to-top print crawl inform us that the Clone Wars, a conflict that originated as a throwaway line in the original 1977 film... http//www.nysun.com/arts/star-wars-clone-wars-painted-in-the-stars/83922/

definitions.definition: Extemporaneous; off the cuff.
definitions.example: The host made a throwaway remark about the president that was later repeated on the television news.

definitions.definition: Selected or used without care or attention.
A French style of abstract painting popular in the 1940s and 1950s.
The act of naming or designating.
That by which anything is denominated or styled; an epithet; a name, designation, or title; especially, a general name indicating a class of like individuals
A class, or society of individuals, called by the same name; a sect or religious subgroup.
definitions.example: She follows the Ahmadiyya denomination of Islam.

definitions.definition: A unit in a series of units of weight, money, etc.
definitions.example: What denomination is that money? They are all 50 euro notes.

definitions.definition: To represent in words.
definitions.example: The feeling is difficult to describe, but not unpleasant.

definitions.definition: To represent by drawing; to draw a plan of; to delineate; to trace or mark out.
definitions.example: to describe a circle by the compasses;   a torch waved about the head in such a way as to describe a circle

definitions.definition: To give rise to a geometrical structure.
definitions.example: The function describes a very complex surface.

definitions.definition: To introduce a new taxon to science by explaining its characteristics and particularly how it differs from other taxa.
definitions.example: The fungus was first described by a botanist.

definitions.definition: To distribute into parts, groups, or classes; to mark off; to class.
A wooden disk having holes through which the lanyard is passed, used for tightening shrouds.
A very accurate marksman.
A penchant for noticing a particular thing, or a person who has such a penchant.
Very accurate in shooting or throwing.
(concerning a stare) Cold; unfriendly.
To undo the process of knowing, to lose knowledge of something.
A variable (usually x, y or z) whose value is to be found.
Any thing, place, or situation about which nothing is known; an unknown fact or piece of information.
A person of no identity; a nonentity
(sometimes postpositive) Not known; unidentified; not well known.
To support from below with props or masonry.
definitions.example: You should underpin the mine roof to prevent further collapse.

definitions.definition: To give support to; to corroborate.
definitions.example: Public confidence in politicians must underpin our democracy.

definitions.definition: A Native American warrior.
A man daring beyond discretion; a bully.
A challenge; a defiance; bravado.
To encounter with courage and fortitude, to defy, to provoke.
definitions.example: After braving tricks on the high-dive, he braved a jump off the first diving platform.

definitions.definition: To adorn; to make fine or showy.
Strong in the face of fear; courageous.
Having any sort of superiority or excellence.
Making a fine show or display.
To lay upon, as an order or command; to give an injunction to; to direct with authority; to order; to charge.
To prohibit or restrain by a judicial order or decree; to put an injunction on.
An art or craft object made by hand.
Manufactured by hand.
definitions.example: handmade shoes

definitions.definition: The removable rigid roof of a convertible or sports car.
A car with such a roof.
An indoor cinema with a roof, as opposed to a drive-in.
From here, from this place, away
definitions.example: Get thee hence, Satan!

definitions.definition: From the living or from this world
definitions.example: After a long battle, my poor daughter was taken hence.

definitions.definition: (of a length of time) in the future from now
definitions.example: A year hence it will be forgotten.

definitions.definition: As a result; therefore, for this reason
definitions.example: I shall go to Japan and hence will not be here in time for the party.

definitions.definition: An exposed plumbing fitting; a tap or spigot; a regulator for controlling the flow of a liquid from a reservoir.
(game development) One or several systems that inject currency into the game's economy, thus controlling or preventing inflation
The superior or head of an abbey or monastery.
definitions.example: The newly appointed abbot decided to take a tour of the abbey with the cardinal's emissary.

definitions.definition: The pastor and/or administrator of an order, including minor and major orders starting with the minor order of porter.
A layman who received the abbey's revenues, after the closing of the monasteries.
(British slang) A brothel-owner's husband or lover.
(British slang) A ponce; a man employed by a prostitute to find clients, and who may also act as a bodyguard or equivalent to a bouncer.
A place from which something can be viewed.
definitions.example: Perched on the edge of the cliff was a hidden outlook.

definitions.definition: The view from such a place.
An attitude or point of view.
definitions.example: He has a positive outlook on life.

definitions.definition: Expectation for the future.
definitions.example: The outlook for temperature rises is worrying.

definitions.definition: To face or look in an outward direction.
To look at (someone) so long or intently that they look away; to win or prevail over (someone or something).
To be more attractive than (someone or something).
To inspect throughly; to select.
To look beyond (something).
A set amount of travelling, seen as a single unit; a discrete trip, a voyage.
Any process or progression likened to a journey, especially one that involves difficulties or personal development.
definitions.example: my journey of dealing with grief

definitions.definition: A day.
A day's travelling; the distance travelled in a day.
A day's work.
The weight of finished coins delivered at one time to the Master of the Mint.
(collective) A group of giraffes.
To travel, to make a trip or voyage.
A short stay somewhere.
A temporary residence.
To reside somewhere temporarily, especially as a guest or lodger.
The fine particles (dust) of wood created by sawing.
To sprinkle with sawdust.
A nonaligned state, or a member of such a state.
A person who takes no side in a dispute.
An individual or entity serving as an arbitrator or adjudicator.
A neutral hue.
The position of a set of gears in which power cannot be transmitted to the drive mechanism.
An electrical terminal or conductor which has zero or close to zero voltage with respect to the ground.
Not taking sides in a conflict such as war; nonaligned.
Favouring neither the supporting nor opposing viewpoint of a topic of debate; unbiased.
definitions.example: I am neutral regarding the issue of gay marriage.

definitions.definition: (grammar) Neither positive nor negative.
Neither beneficial nor harmful.
Having no sex; neuter.
Having no obvious colour; gray
Neither positive nor negative; possessing no charge or equivalent positive and negative charge such that there is no imbalance.
Having a pH near 7, neither acidic nor alkaline.
A person who takes a nap
A sheep stealer
The head
A machine used to raise the nap on cloth
Mischievous; tending to misbehave or act badly (especially of a child).
definitions.example: Some naughty boys at school hid the teacher's lesson notes.

definitions.definition: Sexually provocative; now in weakened sense, risqué, cheeky.
definitions.example: I bought some naughty lingerie for my honeymoon.

definitions.definition: Evil, wicked, morally reprehensible.
Bad, worthless, substandard.
One who roves about for prey; one who prowls.
A new member of an organization.
One who has been through a ceremony of initiation.
To begin; to start.
To instruct in the rudiments or principles; to introduce.
To confer membership on; especially, to admit to a secret order with mysterious rites or ceremonies.
To do the first act; to perform the first rite; to take the initiative.
Unpractised; untried; new.
Begun; commenced; introduced to, or instructed in, the rudiments; newly admitted.
A formation of minerals, specifically:
A large hill or island having no vegetation.
definitions.example: Pearl Rock near Cape Cod is so named because the morning sun makes it gleam like a pearl.

definitions.definition: Something that is strong, stable, and dependable; a person who provides security or support to another.
A lump or cube of ice.
definitions.example: I'll have a whisky on the rocks, please.

definitions.definition: A type of confectionery made from sugar in the shape of a stick, traditionally having some text running through its length.
definitions.example: While we're in Brighton, let's get a stick of rock!

definitions.definition: A crystallized lump of crack cocaine.
An unintelligent person, especially one who repeats mistakes.
An Afrikaner.
An extremely conservative player who is willing to play only the very strongest hands.
Any of several fish:
A basketball.
definitions.example: Yo homie, pass the rock!

definitions.definition: (rock paper scissors) A closed hand (a handshape resembling a rock), that beats scissors and loses to paper. It beats lizard and loses to Spock in rock-paper-scissors-lizard-Spock.
A cricket ball, especially a new one that has not been softened by use
A crystal used to control the radio frequency.
Thought; gerund of think.
definitions.example: What is your thinking on this subject?

definitions.definition: The fastest of the disciplines of alpine skiing.
A rapid descent of a hill in related sports, especially in alpine skiing.
To take part in downhill skiing.
Located down a slope or hill.
Going down a slope or a hill.
(by extension) Easy.
(by extension) Deteriorating, getting worse.
definitions.example: After Don made those tasteless remarks, our relationship with him went downhill.

definitions.definition: Down a slope.
definitions.example: Because we got to the summit of the mountain, we could only go downhill from there.

definitions.definition: The act of deciding.
A choice or judgement.
definitions.example: It is the decision of the court that movies are protected as free speech.

definitions.definition: Firmness of conviction.
definitions.example: After agonizing deliberations, they finally proceeded with decision.

definitions.definition: (chiefly combat sports) A result arrived at by the judges when there is no clear winner at the end of the contest.
definitions.example: He has won twice by knockout, once by decision.

definitions.definition: A win or a loss awarded to a pitcher.
To defeat an opponent by a decision of the judges, rather than by a knockout
A writing tablet consisting of two leaves of rigid material connected by hinges and shutting together so as to protect the writing within.
A picture or series of pictures painted on two tablets, usually connected by hinges.
A double catalogue, containing in one part the names of living, and in the other of deceased, ecclesiastics and benefactors of the church.
A catalogue of saints.
Artistically-wrought tablets distributed by consuls, etc. of the later Roman Empire to commemorate their tenure of office; hence transferred to a list of magistrates
One of several kinds of bird in the family Corvidae
A superficially similar Australian bird, Gymnorhina tibicen or Cracticus tibicen.
Someone who displays a magpie-like quality such as hoarding or stealing objects.
A fan or member of Newcastle United F.C.
The 3rd circle on a target, between the inner and outer.
A halfpenny.
To mark with patches of black and white or light and dark.
To steal or hoard (items) as magpies are believed to do.
To talk idly; to talk about other people's private business.
A non-academic periodical publication, generally consisting of sheets of paper folded in half and stapled at the fold.
An ammunition storehouse.
A chamber in a firearm enabling multiple rounds of ammunition to be fed into the firearm.
A reservoir or supply chamber for a stove, battery, camera, typesetting machine, or other apparatus.
A country or district especially rich in natural products.
A city viewed as a marketing center.
A store, or shop, where goods are kept for sale.
A callow young bird.

A callow or teneral phase of an insect or other arthropod, typically shortly after ecdysis, while the skin still is hardening, the colours have not yet become stable, and as a rule, before the animal is able to move effectively.
An alluvial flat.
Unfledged (of a young bird).
(by extension) Immature, lacking in life experience.
definitions.example: Those three young men are particularly callow youths.

definitions.definition: Lacking color or firmness (of some kinds of insects or other arthropods, such as spiders, just after ecdysis); teneral.
Shallow or weak-willed.
(of a brick) Unburnt.
Of land: low-lying and liable to be submerged.
Bald.
Not realistic.
Having a covering of small knobs.
The flat vertical surface to which the basket is attached.
A flat vertical wall with the image of a tennis net drawn or painted on it. Designed to practice hitting against such that the ball bounces back.
(first aid) A spine board.
A board placed at the back of a cart, boat, etc.
A tradesman who installs, repairs and maintains electrical wiring and equipment.
A scientist who studies electricity.
The ball of the eye
(CB) a meeting
definitions.example: We had an eyeball last year.

definitions.definition: As a synecdoche, a person's focus of attention
(in the plural) readership or viewership
A favourite or pet; the apple of someone's eye.
To gauge, estimate or judge by eye, rather than measuring precisely; to look or glance at.
definitions.example: A good cook can often just eyeball the correct quantities of ingredients.

definitions.definition: To scrutinize
To stare at intently
definitions.example: Are you eyeballing my girl?

definitions.definition: To roll one's eyes.
Extending for the entire duration of life.
definitions.example: They were lifelong friends; they met in elementary school and ended their lives in the same rest home.

definitions.definition: An attractive, lovely person, especially a (professional) beauty.
definitions.example: a calendar depicting young lovelies in bikinis

definitions.definition: Term of fond address.
definitions.example: Goodbye, my lovely.

definitions.definition: A lovely object.
Beautiful; charming; very pleasing in form, looks, tone, or manner.
definitions.example: It's a lovely day and the sun is shining.

definitions.definition: Very nice, wonderful.
definitions.example: It would be lovely to have a little more money to spend.

definitions.definition: Inspiring love or friendship; amiable.
Loving, filled with love.
In a lovely fashion or manner; beautifully.
An obligation, debt or responsibility owed to someone.
A handicap that holds something back, a drawback, someone or something that is a burden to whoever is required to take care of them; an individual or action that exposes others to greater risk.
The likelihood of something happening.
The condition of being susceptible to something.
To close or lock as if with a latch.
To catch; lay hold of.
To smear; to anoint.
(authorship) A dramatic device in which an earlier event is inserted into the normal chronological flow of a narrative.
A vivid mental image of a past trauma, especially one that recurs.
A similar recurrence of the effects of a hallucinogenic drug.
The condition of the flame propagating down the hose of an oxy-fuel welding system.
A query that operates against data from an earlier time, before it was changed.
To undergo a flashback; to experience a vivid mental image from the past.
Size, as measured in acres.
An area of land measured in acres.
Working time outside of one's regular hours.
definitions.example: Workers are usually paid extra for working overtime.

definitions.definition: The rate of pay, usually higher, for work done outside of or in addition to regular hours.
An extra period of play when a contest has a tie score at the end of regulation. (British: extra time.)
definitions.example: That last-second shot ties the game 99-99 and sends it to overtime!

definitions.definition: To measure something incorrectly, as taking more time than it actually did.
Exceeding regular working hours.
Beyond the normal or usual extent.
To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.
definitions.example: Kangaroos are known for their ability to jump high.

definitions.definition: To cause oneself to leave an elevated location and fall downward.
definitions.example: She is going to jump from the diving board.

definitions.definition: To pass by a spring or leap; to overleap.
definitions.example: to jump a stream

definitions.definition: To employ a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
To react to a sudden, often unexpected, stimulus (such as a sharp prick or a loud sound) by jerking the body violently.
definitions.example: The sudden sharp sound made me jump.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.
definitions.example: Share prices jumped by 10% after the company announced record profits.

definitions.definition: To employ a move in certain board games where one game piece is moved from one legal position to another passing over the position of another piece.
definitions.example: The player's knight jumped the opponent's bishop.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.
definitions.example: I hate it when people jump the queue.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: The hoodlum jumped a woman in the alley.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with (a person).
To cause to jump.
definitions.example: The rider jumped the horse over the fence.

definitions.definition: To move the distance between two opposing subjects.
To increase the height of a tower crane by inserting a section at the base of the tower and jacking up everything above it.
To increase speed aggressively and without warning.
To expose to danger; to risk; to hazard.
(smithwork) To join by a buttweld.
To thicken or enlarge by endwise blows; to upset.
(quarrying) To bore with a jumper.
To coincide; to agree; to accord; to tally; followed by with.
To start executing code from a different location, rather than following the program counter.
To flee; to make one's escape.
The act of performing a jump.
Exuberantly active; in full swing.
The surface of the Earth, as opposed to the sky or water or underground.
definitions.example: Look, I found a ten dollar bill on the ground!

definitions.definition: Terrain.
Soil, earth.
definitions.example: The fox escaped from the hounds by going to ground.

definitions.definition: The bottom of a body of water.
Basis, foundation, groundwork, legwork.
(chiefly in the plural) Reason, (epistemic) justification, cause.
definitions.example: He could not come on grounds of health, or on health grounds.

definitions.definition: Background, context, framework, surroundings.
The area on which a battle is fought, particularly as referring to the area occupied by one side or the other. Often, according to the eventualities, "to give ground" or "to gain ground".
(by extension) Advantage given or gained in any contest; e.g. in football, chess, debate or academic discourse.
The plain surface upon which the figures of an artistic composition are set.
definitions.example: crimson flowers on a white ground

definitions.definition: In sculpture, a flat surface upon which figures are raised in relief.
In point lace, the net of small meshes upon which the embroidered pattern is applied.
definitions.example: Brussels ground

definitions.definition: In etching, a gummy substance spread over the surface of a metal to be etched, to prevent the acid from eating except where an opening is made by the needle.
(chiefly in the plural) One of the pieces of wood, flush with the plastering, to which mouldings etc. are attached.
definitions.example: Grounds are usually put up first and the plastering floated flush with them.

definitions.definition: A soccer stadium.
definitions.example: Manchester United's ground is known as Old Trafford.

definitions.definition: An electrical conductor connected to the earth, or a large conductor whose electrical potential is taken as zero (such as a steel chassis).

The area of grass on which a match is played (a cricket field); the entire arena in which it is played; the part of the field behind a batsman's popping crease where he can not be run out (hence to make one's ground).
A composition in which the bass, consisting of a few bars of independent notes, is continually repeated to a varying melody.
The tune on which descants are raised; the plain song.
The pit of a theatre.
To connect (an electrical conductor or device) to a ground.
To punish, especially a child or teenager, by forcing him/her to stay at home and/or give up certain privileges.
definitions.example: Eric, you are grounded until further notice for lying to us about where you were last night!

definitions.definition: To forbid (an aircraft or pilot) to fly.
definitions.example: Because of the bad weather, all flights were grounded.

definitions.definition: To give a basic education in a particular subject; to instruct in elements or first principles.
definitions.example: Jim was grounded in maths.

definitions.definition: To hit a ground ball. Compare fly (verb(regular)) and line (verb).
To place something on the ground.
To run aground; to strike the bottom and remain fixed.
definitions.example: The ship grounded on the bar.

definitions.definition: To found; to fix or set, as on a foundation, reason, or principle; to furnish a ground for; to fix firmly.
To cover with a ground, as a copper plate for etching, or as paper or other materials with a uniform tint as a preparation for ornament.
To improve or focus the mental or emotional state of.
definitions.example: I ground myself with meditation.

definitions.definition: To have a nap; to sleep for a short period of time, especially during the day.
To be off one's guard.
definitions.example: The regulators were caught napping by the financial collapse.

definitions.definition: To form or raise a soft or fuzzy surface on (fabric or leather).
To grab; to nab.
To cover (something) with a sauce (usually in passive).
The act of taking a nap or short sleep.
The act or process of raising a nap, as on cloth.
To incline the head up and down, as to indicate agreement.
To briefly incline the head downwards as a cursory greeting.
To sway, move up and down.
To gradually fall asleep.
To signify by a nod.

definitions.definition: To make a mistake by being temporarily inattentive or tired
To head; to strike the ball with one's head.
definitions.example: Jones nods the ball back to his goalkeeper.

definitions.definition: To allude to something.
To fall asleep while under the influence of opiates.
Something that is variable.
Something whose value may be dictated or discovered.

A symbol representing a variable.
A named memory location in which a program can store intermediate results and from which it can read them.
A variable star.
A shifting wind, or one that varies in force.
(in the plural) Those parts of the sea where a steady wind is not expected, especially the parts between the trade-wind belts.
Able to vary or be varied.

definitions.definition: Likely to vary.
Marked by diversity or difference.
Having no fixed quantitative value.
Tending to deviate from a normal or recognized type.
One who governs a country, province, or colony as the representative of a monarch.

definitions.definition: A zongdu.
An orange and black North American butterfly (Limenitis archippus), so named because it is similar to, but smaller than, the monarch butterfly.
A singer; a person who likes to sing.
To make someone a victim or sacrifice.
To punish someone unjustly.
To swindle or defraud someone.
The winner in a fight or contest.
The letter V in the ICAO spelling alphabet.
A row of cut grain or hay allowed to dry in a field.
A line of leaves etc heaped up by the wind.
A similar streak of seaweed etc on the surface of the sea formed by Langmuir circulation.
A line of snow or gravel left behind by the edge of a snowplow’s or grader’s blade.
The green border of a field, dug up in order to carry the earth on other land to mend it.
To arrange (e.g. new-made hay) in lines or windrows.
To make (something) new again; to restore to freshness or original condition.
To replace (something which has broken etc.); to replenish (something which has been exhausted), to keep up a required supply of.
To make new spiritually; to regenerate.
To become new, or as new; to revive.
To begin again; to recommence.
To repeat.
To extend a period of loan, especially a library book that is due to be returned.

definitions.definition: The act or practice of robbing.
The offense of taking or attempting to take the property of another by force or threat of force.
Any of several large ground-dwelling rodents of the genera Marmota and Cynomys in the squirrel family.
